title: fermented indulgent uruguayan cheese empanadas
description: delicious cheese empanadas featuring a fermented tadka and mexican cheese for a unique and indulgent twist on traditional uruguayan cuisine
instructions: in a large mixing bowl, combine flour, yeast, and salt.
gradually add in water and mix until a dough forms.
knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
preheat the oven to 375 degrees fahrenheit.
on a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
place a tablespoon of mexican cheese in the center of each dough circle.
fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
brush the tops of the empanadas with egg and sprinkle with chili flakes.
bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
let the empanadas cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes before serving.
